# Designing Web APIs (Brenda Jin & Saurabh Sahni & Amir Shevat)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [What’s an API?](.roo/vendor/node/designing-web-apis-brenda-jin-saurabh-sahni-amir-shevat/01-what-s-an-api.md): Web Application Programming Interfaces (APIs), encompassing their conceptualization, design, review, documentation, specification drafting, business logic mapping, architecture determination, and the definition of user stories and personas.
    - [API Paradigms](.roo/vendor/node/designing-web-apis-brenda-jin-saurabh-sahni-amir-shevat/02-api-paradigms.md): Designing, architecting, documenting, refactoring, or implementing Application Programming Interfaces (APIs), including making architectural decisions about API paradigms (REST, RPC, GraphQL, WebHooks, WebSockets, HTTP Streaming), endpoint naming, defining HTTP methods, designing payload structures, or transitioning from polling mechanisms to event-driven architectures.
    - [API Security](.roo/vendor/node/designing-web-apis-brenda-jin-saurabh-sahni-amir-shevat/03-api-security.md): API security architectures, authentication and authorization flows, OAuth 2.0 implementations, and WebHook delivery and verification mechanisms.
    - [Design Best Practices](.roo/vendor/node/designing-web-apis-brenda-jin-saurabh-sahni-amir-shevat/04-design-best-practices.md): The design, implementation, refactoring, documentation, and review of Web APIs, API endpoints, payload structures, API error handling mechanisms, API versioning strategies, and developer onboarding documentation (such as SDKs, tutorials, and guides).
    - [Design in Practice](.roo/vendor/node/designing-web-apis-brenda-jin-saurabh-sahni-amir-shevat/05-design-in-practice.md): Triggers when the user requests assistance with designing, architecting, or specifying a new web API, when defining use cases or user stories for an API, or when generating an API specification document or proposal.
    - [Scaling APIs](.roo/vendor/node/designing-web-apis-brenda-jin-saurabh-sahni-amir-shevat/06-scaling-apis.md): Scaling, optimizing, performance-tuning, paginating, or rate-limiting a Web API; designing strategies to evolve an existing API architecture; or generating/modifying client SDKs to interact with an API.
    - [Managing Change](.roo/vendor/node/designing-web-apis-brenda-jin-saurabh-sahni-amir-shevat/07-managing-change.md): Designing, modifying, updating, or versioning Web APIs, including modifying request/response payloads, adding or removing API endpoints, writing API schema definitions (e.g., JSON Schema, OpenAPI), structuring API routing, or implementing API deprecation and versioning strategies.
    - [Building a Developer Ecosystem Strategy](.roo/vendor/node/designing-web-apis-brenda-jin-saurabh-sahni-amir-shevat/08-building-a-developer-ecosystem-strategy.md): Developer Relations (DevRel) strategy, developer ecosystem plan, API marketing strategy, developer segmentation model, or developer funnel metric system.
    - [Developer Resources](.roo/vendor/node/designing-web-apis-brenda-jin-saurabh-sahni-amir-shevat/09-developer-resources.md): Triggers when the AI is tasked with generating, reviewing, or structuring developer resources, API documentation, developer portals, code samples, API tutorials, SDK/framework designs, API testing environments, or API community engagement materials.
    - [Developer Programs](.roo/vendor/node/designing-web-apis-brenda-jin-saurabh-sahni-amir-shevat/10-developer-programs.md): Developer Programs, developer community engagement, partner engineering, developer ecosystem scaling, hackathons, beta programs, or developer relations strategies.
